authoritative_sources.title: Food Code 2022
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA Food Code is the authoritative U.S. model code for retail food establishments and directly defines restaurant controls for employee health, TCS foods, cleaning, consumer advisories, allergen handling, and disposition boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: restaurant control baseline and hold-trigger mapping
used_for: employee health and contamination stop conditions

authoritative_sources.title: Voluntary National Retail Food Regulatory Program Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's Retail Program Standards are authoritative public guidance for inspection readiness, risk-factor control, complaint response readiness, and corrective-action documentation in retail food programs that oversee restaurants.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: inspection evidence capture and corrective-action review

authoritative_sources.title: National Environmental Assessment Reporting System (NEARS)
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDC is the primary U.S. public-health authority for foodborne illness investigation support. NEARS is authoritative for the environmental assessment factors and evidence collection patterns relevant to restaurant illness incidents.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: illness complaint intake and environmental assessment escalation

authoritative_sources.title: CIFOR Guidelines for Foodborne Disease Outbreak Response
authoritative_sources.publisher: Council to Improve Foodborne Outbreak Response
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CIFOR is a recognized multi-agency foodborne-outbreak framework distributed through CDC stacks and is authoritative for complaint intake, evidence preservation, traceback support, and agency coordination boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: repeat incident review and outbreak-boundary handling
used_for: evidence preservation during suspected outbreak conditions
lane stop conditions for public-health coordination

authoritative_sources.title: A Labeling Guide for Restaurants and Retail Establishments Selling Away-From-Home Foods Part II
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's menu-labeling guidance is the authoritative federal reference for covered restaurant menu information duties and helps define where safety work intersects with guest-facing menu disclosures and chain-level labeling obligations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: menu, order, and guest-information boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. federal consumer-finance regulator for billing-error guidance. This source is authoritative for payment mismatch, refund posting, and chargeback-sensitive customer routing boundaries when restaurant safety cases touch card disputes.

authoritative_sources.title: PCI DSS
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized standards body for payment-card security, and PCI DSS is the authoritative industry control framework for handling restaurant payment artifacts when fraud-sensitive or card-data-sensitive records are part of the case.

workflow_stages.name: request intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm restaurant unit, jurisdiction, concept, order or reservation context, affected menu item, guest impact, payment posture, and whether the request stays inside restaurant food-safety assurance before proceeding.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: control and prerequisite mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map the case to the exact restaurant controls, logs, hold tags, allergen steps, employee-health records, menu information, and incident prerequisites needed before service, reopening, or closure can be recommended.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: evidence assembly and inspection review
workflow_stages.description: Collect and evaluate POS tickets, reservation and guest records, production records, temperature logs, sanitation checks, incident notes, inspection findings, refund records, and chargeback references needed to support a disposition.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: hold, release, and service-continuation boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether visible evidence supports maintaining a product or station hold, recommending continued service with controls, escalating an investigation, or blocking release without implying final authority.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: incident, repeat-defect, and policy-exception escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate recurring deviations, suspected foodborne illness, contamination or tampering complaints, payment disputes, guest-recovery demands, and conflicting tenant or public rules that materially change the safe answer.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: POS checks, online and marketplace orders, kitchen display tickets, reservation records, and guest profile or complaint records
receiving logs, ingredient labels, prep sheets, lot or date-code references, production sheets, line checks, hot and cold holding logs, cooling logs, reheating logs, and sanitizer checks
allergen matrices, recipe specifications, employee illness reports, exclusion or restriction logs, cleaning checklists, and vomit or diarrheal event cleanup records
hold tags, disposition notes, corrective-action trackers, inspection reports, internal audit findings, and incident reports
payment tenders, refund ledgers, fee-waiver requests, chargeback references, and third-party delivery case records
systems_of_record: food-safety log repository or back-office operations platform

allowed: Classify restaurant food-safety requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Prepare evidence-backed safety summaries, hold or nonconformance records, incident summaries, and escalation packets without overstating authority.
Determine whether visible evidence supports continued hold, investigation, corrective-action escalation, or a release recommendation subject to external approval.
escalate: Required order, payment, production, temperature, employee-health, inspection, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires medical advice, public-health management, final release authority, pricing or refund approval, fraud decisioning, or legal interpretation.
A public baseline and tenant, franchise, platform, or jurisdiction rule materially conflict.
Customer harm, suspected foodborne illness, tampering, allergen exposure, or payment-fraud implications change whether service may proceed or the case may close.
refuse: Clear product, a station, or a finding when required logs, hold records, corrective actions, or incident evidence are missing or contradictory.
Override pricing, refund, comp, chargeback, or safety policies without explicit authority.
Diagnose guests, claim outbreak closure, or provide regulator-facing assurances.

domain_failure_modes: Treating a guest illness complaint as ordinary service recovery and closing the case without preserving temperature, production, and employee-health evidence.
Allowing product or a station to return to service even though hold tags, temperature logs, cleaning records, or corrective actions are missing, stale, or contradictory.
Collapsing high-volume line-check exceptions into generic operational noise instead of answer-changing safety evidence.
Treating menu-labeling, refunds, fee waivers, or chargebacks as food-safety decisions the lane can approve.
Ignoring the difference between FDA Food Code model guidance and the tenant's actual local health-code adoption or franchise SOPs.
Failing to link order, reservation, kitchen, and payment records when an allergen, contamination, or third-party delivery complaint spans multiple systems.
Producing generic hospitality advice that ignores restaurant-specific control vocabulary such as TCS, date marking, reheating, cross-contact, or employee exclusion.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Safety or quality evidence is incomplete or contradictory
Release is requested while a hold or unresolved issue exists
Incident handling requires licensed, safety, or formal QA authority

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific adoption of FDA Food Code provisions
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: FDA Food Code is a model code rather than directly binding law, so each tenant must supply the actual state or local health-code adoption and any stricter local rules before a case-specific legal conclusion is safe.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Restaurant menu-labeling coverage and franchise thresholds
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Federal menu-labeling requirements do not apply identically to every restaurant format, so the tenant must supply whether the brand, franchise, or concept is covered before menu-labeling obligations determine the answer.
